figures.caption: Figure 1. A basic illustration of current mainstream AFT methods and our AdvFLYP paradigm. Rather than performing AFT on a well-curated proxy dataset via a cross-entropy loss, AdvFLYP adversarially finetunes the vision encoder on web-scale image-text pairs via a contrastive loss.
Table 11. Classification accuracy (%) on 14 downstream datasets tested with three adversarial attack algorithms (ϵ = 4/255). We highlight the best and second best result.
Table 1. Classification accuracy (%) on 14 downstream datasets tested with three adversarial attack algorithms. We highlight the best and second best result.
Table 2. Accuracy (%) on clean images of 14 downstream datasets.
Table 10. Classification accuracy (%) on 14 downstream datasets tested with three adversarial attack algorithms (ϵ = 2/255). We highlight the best and second best result.
Table 3. Adversarial robustness under stronger attack budget ϵ. The reported values are accuracy averaged over 14 datasets.
Table 13. The clean accuracy of the models finetuned on 100k image-text web data and a 100k-subset of ImageNet, respectively. Both baselines are finetuned with clean non-adversarial images.
Table 14. Robustness of the models finetuned on class-labelled ImageNet (PMG-AFT), VLM-captioned ImageNet (AFTcapIN ), and our AdvFLYPfull, evaluated with AutoAttack (ϵ = 1/255).
Figure 3. Performance of AdvFLYPfull averaged over 14 downstream datasets versus the amount of image-text pairs from the web. The robust accuracy is evaluated under PGD-10 (ϵ = 1/255).

Figure 2. Overview of the formulation of LCLIP , logit-level regularisation Llogit and feature-level regularisation Lfeat. LCLIP is the main loss of AdvFLYP (Eq. (7)). Llogit and Lfeat are only employed in the regularised variant of AdvFLYP (Eq. (13)), denoted as AdvFLYPfull.
Figure 4. An example of an image from ImageNet and its description generated by Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct.
